experiment: F-ECO5 UCB1 vs heuristic dispatch comparison
hypothesis: UCB1 (single parameter c=1.414) produces fundamentally different rankings than heuristic (13 constants), favoring underexplored domains
method: Run both scoring modes on 540 dispatches across 36 domains. Compare rankings via Spearman rho, Kendall tau, top-5 overlap. Simulate 10-dispatch Gini trajectory.

ranking_correlation.interpretation: Zero correlation — rankings are completely independent

top5_overlap.interpretation: Heuristic rewards structural richness (many ISOs/lessons). UCB1 rewards underexploration + yield.

fluid_dynamics.reason: 1 dispatch, 2 lessons = highest yield + explore bonus

gini_simulation.note: Modest improvement — 10 dispatches out of 540 total is marginal

ucb1_strengths: Correctly deprioritizes over-exploited domains (meta n=79 → rank 31)
Naturally handles exploration-exploitation without stacked heuristics
Unvisited domains get maximum priority (formally correct)
Single parameter c replaces 13 hand-tuned constants
ucb1_weaknesses: At n=1, exploit estimate is extremely noisy (fluid-dynamics: 2.0 L/dispatch from 1 sample)
Ignores structural information (ISO count, frontier count, concept types)
No temporal recency — dispatch 50 sessions ago = dispatch last session
Lessons-per-dispatch may not capture quality (high-K lessons vs low-K)
conclusions.recommendation: UCB1 as primary scoring mode with structural tiebreaker for unvisited domains. Keep 20% hard floor for domains with <3 visits. Heuristic constants archived.
conclusions.verdict: L-697 CONFIRMED — UCB1 produces radically different (rho=0.017), formally justified rankings. Heuristic's 4 rounds of fixes (L-621/625/671/676) were local optima chase; UCB1 is the global solution.

predictions_for_validation.p3: Coverage >= 90% of domains visited within 30 sessions
